description: Add `task-mgr invalidate-learning <id>` command with two-step degradation: first call downgrades confidence to Low, second call (already Low) sets retired_at to soft-archive the learning.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: high confidence learning -> downgraded to low (action='downgraded')
Test: medium confidence learning -> downgraded to low (action='downgraded')
Test: low confidence learning -> retired (action='retired', retired_at set)
Test: two-step sequence (high -> low -> retired in two calls)
Test: already-retired learning returns InvalidState error
Test: non-existent learning ID returns NotFound error
Test: format_text produces correct output for both downgrade and retire cases
Known-bad discriminator: test that confidence=Low on first call returns 'downgraded' not 'retired' (rejects naive 'Low always retires' stub)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation defining expected behavior for the invalidate-learning two-step degradation. Tests should compile but fail (or be #[ignore]) until implementation exists. Place in src/commands/invalidate_learning.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests.
edgeCases: Learning already retired: must return InvalidState error, not silently succeed
Learning not found: must return NotFound error with learning ID
Confidence already Low on first call: must still return 'downgraded' action (not 'retired'), writes Low via edit_learning idempotently
Two rapid sequential calls: first sets Low, second retires — correct ordering guaranteed by single-threaded CLI + LockGuard

failureModes.cause: Learning ID does not exist in database
Learning is already retired (retired_at IS NOT NULL)

invariants: A single invalidate call never both downgrades AND retires in one operation
After downgrade, confidence is always Low regardless of starting level
After retirement, retired_at IS NOT NULL in the database
format_text output always contains the learning title and ID
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Use setup_db() from crate::learnings::test_helpers and RecordLearningParams from crate::learnings::crud. The Learning struct does NOT have retired_at, so verify retirement via direct SQL: SELECT retired_at FROM learnings WHERE id = ?1. Follow apply_learning.rs test patterns exactly.
userStories.title: Initial tests for invalidate_learning command

acceptanceCriteria: InvalidateLearningResult struct with fields: learning_id (i64), title (String), previous_confidence (String), action (String), new_confidence (Option<String>)
InvalidateLearningResult derives Debug, Clone, Serialize
invalidate_learning(conn: &Connection, learning_id: i64) -> TaskMgrResult<InvalidateLearningResult>
get_learning returns None -> TaskMgrError::learning_not_found(id)
retired_at IS NOT NULL -> TaskMgrError::InvalidState error
confidence != Low -> edit_learning with confidence: Some(Confidence::Low), action='downgraded'
confidence == Low -> UPDATE learnings SET retired_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?1, action='retired'
format_text: downgrade -> 'Invalidated learning #ID: "Title" (confidence: prev -> low)'
format_text: retire -> 'Retired learning #ID: "Title" (was already low confidence)'
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.description: Create src/commands/invalidate_learning.rs with InvalidateLearningResult struct, invalidate_learning() function, and format_text(). Follow apply_learning.rs structural template exactly. Logic: fetch learning via get_learning -> check not already retired via SELECT retired_at -> if confidence != Low: downgrade via edit_learning -> if confidence == Low: retire via UPDATE SET retired_at = datetime('now').
userStories.notes: Key detail: Learning struct lacks retired_at field, so use direct SQL query SELECT retired_at FROM learnings WHERE id = ?1 to check retirement status. Use crate::learnings::crud::{get_learning, edit_learning, EditLearningParams} and crate::models::Confidence. Match apply_learning.rs structure: result struct at top, main function, format_text, #[cfg(test)] mod tests.

correctness: First invalidation MUST set confidence to Low regardless of current level
Second invalidation (already Low) MUST set retired_at via SQL
Already-retired learnings MUST return InvalidState error
Non-existent IDs MUST return NotFound error via learning_not_found()
performance: Best effort — single-row operations on indexed primary key
style: Use edit_learning() for confidence downgrade (no raw SQL for CRUD-covered fields)
Use raw SQL only for retired_at update (matching curate retire pattern)

userStories.title: Implement invalidate_learning core logic and result struct

src/main.rs: Commands::InvalidateLearning { learning_id } match arm with LockGuard::acquire, open_connection, invalidate_learning call, output_result
src/main.rs: import invalidate_learning in the use task_mgr::commands block
src/handlers.rs: impl_text_formattable!(InvalidateLearningResult, format_invalidate_learning_text) added
src/handlers.rs: InvalidateLearningResult and format_invalidate_learning_text added to imports from crate::commands

userStories.description: Wire the new invalidate_learning module into the full CLI pipeline: 1) src/commands/mod.rs: add pub mod + re-exports, 2) src/cli/commands.rs: add InvalidateLearning variant, 3) src/main.rs: add dispatch match arm with LockGuard, 4) src/handlers.rs: add impl_text_formattable! macro line.
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern from ApplyLearning wiring: same LockGuard + open_connection + output_result pattern in main.rs dispatch. output_result called with cli.format for both JSON and text support
